response.justification: Strong senior software engineer with 15+ years of experience across diverse stacks, architecture exposure, open-source contributions, and Linux/Python-adjacent work. However, the role requires German at C1 minimum and the candidate reports only Basic German, which is a hard blocker for a client-facing leadership position in a German-speaking consulting context. Formal leadership/people-management experience is also not clearly demonstrated — most roles are IC senior/staff engineer rather than head-of-development. Worth a conversation only if German proficiency is negotiable or has improved.
strengths: Extensive senior engineering experience across web, embedded, and distributed systems with strong open-source and Linux background
Demonstrated architecture, technical leadership, and mentoring through workshops, talks, and founding GISCollective
Proven track record with modern stacks, TDD, CI/CD, and craftsmanship practices aligned with consulting work
concerns: German is only Basic — the role explicitly requires C1, which is a hard requirement for client advisory
No explicit head-of-development or formal team-management experience; leadership is largely informal/founder-level
Python and UML are listed as desired but are not evident in the resume; primary stacks are TypeScript/Node and DLang

key_factors.factor: German language proficiency (C1 required, candidate Basic)
Senior engineering breadth and open-source/Linux orientation
